question_claim.claim: MCPs must collect and report HEDIS data in compliance with CMS and DHCS requirements.
question_claim.source_text: HEDIS data collection and reporting shall comply with CMS and DHCS requirements

best_match.rationale: Section II.A explicitly requires HEDIS data collection and reporting for Medi-Cal and OneCare to comply with CMS and DHCS requirements. This is near-verbatim coverage of the regulator's ask — the P&P phrases the obligation as a noun clause where the questionnaire phrases it as a verbal requirement, but the scope and subject match exactly.

policy_claim.claim: HEDIS data collection and reporting for Medi-Cal and OneCare must comply with CMS and DHCS requirements.
policy_claim.source_text: HEDIS® data collection and reporting for Medi-Cal, and OneCare programs shall comply with CMS and DHCS requirements

question_claim.claim: The Plan must name a primary and back-up HEDIS lead who serves as liaison to DHCS.
question_claim.source_text: a designated and back-up HEDIS lead

best_match.rationale: Procedure §III.A(2) directs Quality Analytics to provide DHCS with a designated and back-up contact acting as HEDIS lead and DHCS liaison. This covers the questionnaire on both counts — primary contact and back-up — and the liaison framing matches what a regulator expects to see when naming a point of accountability.

policy_claim.claim: The Plan provides DHCS with a designated and back-up HEDIS lead contact.
policy_claim.source_text: Provide DHCS with one (1) designated and back-up contact to serve as CalOptima Health’s HEDIS® lead

question_claim.claim: Minor, non-substantive revisions to the Compliance Plan require prior Board of Directors approval.
question_claim.source_text: minor, non-substantive revisions to the Compliance Plan require Board of Directors approval

contradictions.rationale: Section II.F explicitly allows minor, non-substantive revisions WITHOUT Board of Directors approval — directly opposite to what the questionnaire requires. The P&P carve-out should be reconciled with APL 23-030 before the next audit.

policy_claim.claim: The Compliance Officer may make minor, non-substantive revisions to the Compliance Plan without Board of Directors approval.
policy_claim.source_text: may update and make minor, non-substantive revisions to the Compliance Plan without the need to obtain Board of Directors approval

question_claim.claim: The Compliance Officer must be independent and must not serve in both a compliance and operational role.
question_claim.source_text: the Compliance Officer is considered independent and does not serve in both a compliance and operational role

best_match.rationale: Section II.E.b uses effectively the same language the questionnaire requires: full-time, independent, and explicitly prohibited from dual compliance and operational roles. The stance is definitional rather than aspirational, which is exactly what a regulator auditing this line of the questionnaire would expect to see in a Compliance Program P&P.

policy_claim.claim: The Compliance Officer is a full-time, independent employee and does not serve in both a compliance and operational role.
policy_claim.source_text: The Compliance Officer is a full-time employee of CalOptima Health and is considered independent, which means they do not serve in both a compliance and operational role

question_claim.claim: Employees reporting suspected compliance violations must be protected from retaliation.
question_claim.source_text: protect employees reporting suspected compliance violations from retaliation

question_claim.claim: The Compliance Program must be reviewed and updated at least annually.
question_claim.source_text: shall refresh the Compliance Program at minimum once per year

best_match.rationale: Section II.C covers the requirement — the Compliance Program is reviewed and updated “no less than annually” — but the phrasing drifts from the questionnaire's “refresh... once per year.” Same cadence, different wording.

policy_claim.claim: The Compliance Program is reviewed and updated no less than annually.
policy_claim.source_text: CalOptima Health shall review and update the Compliance Program, including the Compliance Plan
